skill.name: character-development-assistant
skill.description: Creates structured character profiles with appearance, personality, backstory, goals, relationships, and character arcs for fiction writers.
skill.summary: Builds detailed character profiles and development arcs for stories.
skill.author: AbdulSamad94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found Markdown formatting and normal fiction-writing language. No executable code, network calls, filesystem access, environment access, or prompt injection attempts were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged backtick fences used to show Markdown output examples. These lines are documentation formatting, not Ruby shell execution or executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are opening or closing Markdown code fences around sample character profiles. There is no interpreter directive, command string, or execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Writing Terms Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary prose such as descriptions, desires, and Markdown profile headings. No cryptographic function, hash library, cipher call, or security-sensitive operation appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file contains a creative-writing guide and sample prose only. The flagged terms do not appear in code and cannot create a cryptographic weakness.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Fiction Context Flagged as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts occur in character examples and writing tips. They describe fictional relationships, beliefs, work habits, and personality contrasts, not host inspection or system probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are narrative content inside examples and guidance. No commands, host identifiers, directory paths, or data collection instructions are present.

content.value_statement: Flat characters make stories harder to believe and harder to finish. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured character profiles and arcs.
actual_capabilities: Builds complete character profiles with appearance, personality, backstory, and voice.
Defines external goals, internal needs, fears, desires, and secrets.
Maps character relationships, conflicts, and growth opportunities.
Creates positive, negative, or flat character arcs.
Provides validation checks for believable and useful characters.
limitations: It does not write a full manuscript or complete scene draft.
It needs user input about genre, role, and story function for best results.
It does not verify cultural, historical, or medical accuracy.
It may require revision to match an existing story bible.

use_cases.title: Create a New Protagonist
use_cases.description: Develop a main character with clear goals, flaws, relationships, and a complete transformation arc.

use_cases.title: Deepen a Supporting Character
use_cases.description: Turn a secondary character into a believable person with history, motivation, and story purpose.

use_cases.title: Fix a Flat Character
use_cases.description: Identify missing conflict, weak motivation, or unclear growth, then rebuild the character profile.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Character Profile
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new character.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a character profile for a [role] in a [genre] story. The character concept is [brief concept]. Include appearance, personality, backstory, goals, and arc.

prompt_templates.title: Develop Motivation and Conflict
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate work on goals and internal pressure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me deepen [character name]. Their external goal is [goal]. Build their internal need, greatest fear, deepest desire, secret, and main conflict.

prompt_templates.title: Map Character Relationships
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced relationship planning across a cast.
prompt_templates.prompt: Map the relationships between [character name] and these characters: [list]. For each one, define the dynamic, conflict point, and growth opportunity.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Character Arc
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced revision for an existing draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this character arc: [summary]. Identify weak points, missing turning points, unclear motivation, and changes needed for a stronger transformation.

output_examples.input: I need a young mage protagonist who discovers forbidden magic.
output_examples.output: A structured profile with the mage's appearance, hidden fear, family history, secret power, rival relationship, and self-acceptance arc.

output_examples.input: My romance protagonist needs a best friend with their own problems.
output_examples.output: A supporting character profile with emotional strengths, avoidance flaws, advice-giving role, personal subplot, and relationship tension.

output_examples.input: My antagonist feels generic and only wants power.
output_examples.output: A revised antagonist concept with a personal wound, principled goal, moral blind spot, pressure points, and a meaningful connection to the protagonist.

best_practices: Provide the character role, genre, and story function before asking for a full profile.
Use the profile as a draft, then revise details to match your plot.
Check that each flaw creates conflict in scenes, not only description.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a perfect character without flaws or hard choices.
Do not treat personality frameworks as fixed rules for behavior.
Do not add backstory details that never affect the story.

faq.question: Can this skill create a complete character sheet?
faq.answer: Yes. It creates a structured profile with appearance, personality, backstory, motivations, relationships, and character arc.

faq.question: Does it work for any genre?
faq.answer: Yes. It can support fantasy, science fiction, romance, historical fiction, contemporary stories, and other narrative genres.

faq.question: Can it improve an existing character?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the current character notes and ask for gaps, stronger motivations, or a clearer arc.

faq.question: Does it write scenes?
faq.answer: No. The skill focuses on character planning, profile structure, and development guidance.

faq.question: Can it create relationships between characters?
faq.answer: Yes. It maps relationship type, dynamic, conflict points, and opportunities for growth.

faq.question: Is it useful for collaborative writing?
faq.answer: Yes. The structured profile helps teams discuss character choices with shared terms.
